requirements.type: rank | attribute | trait | edge | wild_card | arcane_background | hindrance_any_of | special

effects.type: modifier | resource | reroll | restriction | grant | damage | action | status | special
effects.operator: add | subtract | set | ignore | grant | reroll | draw | recover | replace | double | halve | fail | break
effects.details: human-readable clarification

entries.name: All Thumbs
entries.summary: The hero is bad with mechanical or electrical devices.

effects.details: Applies to Trait rolls made while using mechanical or electrical devices.
A critical failure can break the device.

entries.name: Anemic
entries.summary: The hero is especially vulnerable to fatigue and related hazards.

effects.details: Subtract 2 from Vigor rolls made to resist Fatigue.

entries.name: Arrogant
entries.summary: The hero believes they are the best and wants to prove it.
notes: Roleplaying Hindrance that pushes the character to challenge the greatest threat and dominate opponents.

entries.name: Bad Eyes
entries.summary: Poor eyesight reduces performance on vision-dependent tasks.

effects.details: Subtract 1 from any Trait roll dependent on vision.
Subtract 2 from any Trait roll dependent on vision.
If glasses are lost or broken in a setting where they exist, the character is Distracted.
If glasses are lost or broken in a setting where they exist, the character is also Vulnerable.

notes: Glasses negate the vision penalty when worn in settings where they are available.

entries.name: Bad Luck
entries.summary: The hero begins each session with less luck than normal.

effects.details: The character gets one fewer Benny per session than normal.

notes: A character cannot have both Bad Luck and the Luck Edge.

entries.name: Big Mouth
entries.summary: The hero has trouble keeping secrets.
notes: Roleplaying Hindrance that causes plans or private information to be revealed at bad times.

entries.name: Blind
entries.summary: The hero is completely blind.

effects.details: Subtract 6 from all physical tasks that require sight.
Gain one free Edge as compensation.

entries.name: Bloodthirsty
entries.summary: The hero does not take prisoners unless strictly supervised.
notes: Roleplaying Hindrance that creates enemies, loses information, and causes trouble with allies or authorities.

entries.name: Can't Swim
entries.summary: The hero is especially poor at swimming.

effects.details: Subtract 2 from Athletics when swimming.
Each inch moved in water costs 3 inches of Pace.

entries.name: Cautious
entries.summary: The hero avoids rash action and overplans.
notes: Roleplaying Hindrance focused on restraint and careful planning.

entries.name: Clueless
entries.summary: The hero misses obvious facts and surroundings.

effects.details: Subtract 1 from Common Knowledge rolls.
Subtract 1 from Notice rolls.

entries.name: Clumsy
entries.summary: The hero is badly uncoordinated.

effects.details: Subtract 2 from Athletics rolls.
Subtract 2 from Stealth rolls.

entries.name: Code of Honor
entries.summary: The hero follows a strict moral code.
notes: Roleplaying Hindrance centered on keeping one’s word and avoiding cruel or dishonorable conduct.

entries.name: Curious
entries.summary: The hero must investigate secrets and mysteries.
notes: Roleplaying Hindrance that pushes the character to explore dangerous unknowns.

entries.name: Death Wish
entries.summary: The hero will take extreme risks in pursuit of a noble but deadly goal.
notes: Roleplaying Hindrance; not suicidal, but willing to risk everything for the goal.

entries.name: Delusional
entries.summary: The hero strongly believes something bizarre or false.
notes: Minor Delusions are mostly private or harmless.
Major Delusions are voiced openly and can create danger.

entries.name: Doubting Thomas
entries.summary: The hero rationalizes away the supernatural.
notes: Roleplaying Hindrance that makes the character slow to accept supernatural danger even after seeing it.

entries.name: Driven
entries.summary: The hero is consumed by a personal ambition.
notes: Minor Drive shapes decisions but is infrequent or fairly harmless.
Major Drive is overriding and regularly creates danger.

entries.name: Elderly
entries.summary: Age slows the hero physically but grants extra learned skill.

effects.details: Reduce Pace by 1.
Subtract 1 from running rolls, minimum 1.
Subtract 1 from Agility rolls, but not linked skills.
Subtract 1 from Strength rolls and Strength-based damage, but not linked skills.
Subtract 1 from Vigor rolls, but not linked skills.
Gain 5 extra skill points for Smarts-linked skills.

entries.name: Enemy
entries.summary: A foe wants the hero ruined, imprisoned, or dead.
notes: Severity depends on how powerful and active the enemy is.
If the enemy is defeated permanently, the GM should replace it or the Hindrance may be bought off with an Advance.

entries.name: Greedy
entries.summary: The hero covets wealth and possessions.
notes: Minor Greed causes constant arguments over treasure.
Major Greed can lead to serious harm over wealth or unfairness.

entries.name: Habit
entries.summary: The hero has a compulsion or addiction.

effects.details: An addict who goes without the habit must make a Vigor roll every 24 hours or take a level of Fatigue.

notes: A Healing roll with proper medicine can remove one withdrawal Fatigue level for four hours, after which it returns unless the character gets the addictive substance.

entries.name: Hard of Hearing
entries.summary: The hero has serious hearing loss or is deaf.

effects.details: Subtract 4 from all Notice rolls made to hear, including waking from noise.
The character is deaf and automatically fails all hearing-based Notice rolls.

notes: Hearing aids reduce the penalty by 2 but can fall out on trauma.

entries.name: Heroic
entries.summary: The hero always comes to the aid of those in need.
notes: Roleplaying Hindrance that consistently pushes the character into dangerous rescue situations.

entries.name: Hesitant
entries.summary: The hero falters in stressful situations.

effects.details: Draw two Action Cards and act on the lower one.
Characters with Hesitant cannot take Quick or Level Headed.

entries.name: Illiterate
entries.summary: The hero cannot properly read or write.
notes: Cannot read or write any language, regardless of languages spoken.

entries.name: Impulsive
entries.summary: The hero acts before thinking things through.
notes: Roleplaying Hindrance that favors hasty action.

entries.name: Jealous
entries.summary: The hero envies others and resents being outshined.
notes: Minor Jealousy centers on one subject.
Major Jealousy is broad and often becomes sabotage or slander.

entries.name: Loyal
entries.summary: The hero stands by friends and risks much for them.
notes: Roleplaying Hindrance that prioritizes allies over personal safety.

entries.name: Mean
entries.summary: The hero is disagreeable and struggles to be kind.

effects.details: Subtract 1 from Persuasion rolls.

entries.name: Mild Mannered
entries.summary: The hero has trouble appearing threatening.

effects.details: Subtract 2 from Intimidation rolls.

entries.name: Mute
entries.summary: The hero cannot speak.
notes: The character must communicate through writing, sign language, or other visual methods.

entries.name: Obese
entries.summary: The hero carries excess weight in a way that hinders mobility.

effects.details: Increase Size by 1, which also increases Toughness by 1.
Reduce Pace by 1.
Reduce the running die by one die type, minimum d4.
Treat Strength as one die type lower for armor and worn gear, but not weapons.

notes: Cannot be combined with Brawny.
This Hindrance cannot raise Size above +3.

entries.name: Obligation
entries.summary: The hero has a recurring responsibility that consumes time.
notes: A Minor Obligation takes about 20 hours most weeks.
A Major Obligation takes 40 or more hours most weeks.

entries.name: One Arm
entries.summary: The hero has only one usable arm.

effects.details: Subtract 4 from tasks that require two hands, such as some Athletics rolls or using a two-handed weapon.

entries.name: One Eye
entries.summary: Depth perception is impaired by the loss of one eye.

effects.details: Subtract 2 from vision-dependent Trait rolls against targets more than 5 inches away.

entries.name: Outsider
entries.summary: The hero is an obvious social outsider.

effects.details: Subtract 2 from Persuasion rolls against those outside the hero’s own kind.

notes: The Major version also means the hero has few or no legal rights in the main campaign area.

entries.name: Overconfident
entries.summary: The hero believes they can overcome any challenge.
notes: Roleplaying Hindrance that discourages retreat and pushes the character into risks.

entries.name: Pacifist
entries.summary: The hero avoids harming others, especially living foes.
notes: Minor Pacifists fight only when forced and forbid killing helpless victims.
Major Pacifists do not fight living, sapient beings except in defense and with nonlethal methods.

entries.name: Phobia
entries.summary: An irrational fear undermines the hero in its presence.

effects.details: Subtract 1 from all Trait rolls when confronted by the phobia.
Subtract 2 from all Trait rolls when confronted by the phobia.

entries.name: Poverty
entries.summary: The hero starts poor and struggles to keep money.

effects.details: Start with half the usual setting funds.
In general, total funds are halved every game week.

entries.name: Quirk
entries.summary: The hero has a small but troublesome personal foible.
notes: Usually roleplaying-focused, though the quirk should sometimes create complications.

entries.name: Ruthless
entries.summary: The hero will go very far to achieve a goal.
notes: Minor Ruthlessness stops short of serious harm except against direct opposition.
Major Ruthlessness harms anyone who stands in the way.

entries.name: Secret
entries.summary: The hero is hiding something dangerous or damaging.
notes: If the secret becomes public, it should typically become a different Hindrance such as Enemy, Shamed, or Wanted.

entries.name: Shamed
entries.summary: The hero bears a disgrace from the past.
notes: Minor Shame is private and internal.
Major Shame is known to others and can be used against the hero.

entries.name: Slow
entries.summary: The hero’s mobility is reduced by disability or past injury.

effects.details: Reduce Pace by 1.
Reduce the running die one step.
Reduce Pace by 2.
Reduce the running die one step.
Subtract 2 from Athletics rolls.
Subtract 2 from rolls to resist Athletics-based effects such as Tests or Grappling.
Slow characters may not take Fleet-Footed.

notes: A Minor Slow character may have a prosthesis; if it is lost, treat them as Major Slow.
Wheelchair options are available in later-tech settings.

entries.name: Small
entries.summary: The hero is especially small or frail.

effects.details: Reduce Size by 1.
Reduce Toughness by 1, even if Size cannot go below −1.

entries.name: Stubborn
entries.summary: The hero refuses to yield or admit mistakes.
notes: Roleplaying Hindrance centered on insisting on one’s own way.

entries.name: Suspicious
entries.summary: The hero distrusts others.

effects.details: Support rolls made to aid the character are made at −2.

notes: Minor Suspicion is mostly roleplaying-driven distrust and paranoia.

entries.name: Thin Skinned
entries.summary: Personal attacks hit the hero especially hard.

effects.details: Subtract 2 when resisting Taunt attacks.
Subtract 4 when resisting Taunt attacks.

entries.name: Timid
entries.summary: The hero is squeamish and easily cowed.

effects.details: Subtract 2 from Fear checks.
Subtract 2 when resisting Intimidation.

entries.name: Tongue-Tied
entries.summary: The hero routinely says the wrong thing.

effects.details: Subtract 1 from speech-based Intimidation rolls.
Subtract 1 from speech-based Performance rolls.
Subtract 1 from speech-based Persuasion rolls.
Subtract 1 from speech-based Taunt rolls.

entries.name: Ugly
entries.summary: The hero’s appearance makes social influence harder.

effects.details: Subtract 1 from Persuasion rolls.
Subtract 2 from Persuasion rolls.

entries.name: Vengeful
entries.summary: The hero always seeks payback.
notes: Minor Vengefulness usually seeks lawful or limited revenge.
Major Vengefulness escalates until the character feels fully satisfied.

entries.name: Vow
entries.summary: The hero has sworn an oath that makes demands on time and risk.
notes: Severity depends on how dangerous and demanding the oath is.

entries.name: Wanted
entries.summary: Authorities are looking for the hero over a crime.
notes: Severity depends on the seriousness of the crime and how active the pursuit is.

entries.name: Young
entries.summary: The hero is notably young, with fewer starting points but more luck.

effects.details: A Young hero gets 4 attribute points instead of 5.
A Young hero gets 10 skill points instead of 12.
A Young hero draws one extra Benny at the start of each session.
A Very Young hero gets 3 attribute points instead of 5.
A Very Young hero gets 10 skill points.
A Very Young hero also has the Small Hindrance.
A Very Young hero draws two extra Bennies at the start of each session.

notes: Young heroes may also face setting-specific legal restrictions such as driving or weapon ownership.
Most Minor Young characters should also take Small, but it is not mandatory.
